content_en:
At-Tarmidhi also reports ~Uqbah Ibn ~Amir to have said, "Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, said, ‘If there be a Prophet after me, that would be “Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab.' "

Ibn “Umar narrates, "The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said, ' ‘Umar will be the "bright moon" among the residents of Paradise.’ “ (Reported by Al-Bazzaar.)

Anas Ibn Malik and ~ Ali Ibn Abu Taalib, may Allah be pleased with them both, narrate that Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, said to Abu Bakr and * Umar: "Those two will be the chiefs of the old men in Paradise, both from old and modern times, excluding messengers and prophets... Do not tell them of that, “Ali.” (Reported by AtTarmidhi.)

*Aa’ishah, may Allah be pleased with her, is reported to have said, “ Satan fears ~ Umar." (Reported by Ibn ~ Aasaakir.)

Ibn ~Abbaas, may Allah be pleased with them both, narrates, "Gabriel came to the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, and said, ‘Give “Umar Allah's greetings and tell him that his rage (when Allah is disobeyed) only boosts Islam, and his satisfaction (with something said or done) asserts that it is correct.’ " (Reported by At-Tabarani.)

Ibn ~Abbaas, may Allah be pleased with them both,

further reports that the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said, "Every angel in heaven reveres “Umar, and every devil on earth fears him." (Reported by Ibn ~ Aasaakir.)

Al-Fadl, Ibn ~ Abbaas reports that the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said, "After my decease, the truth shall be with “Umar wherever he may be." (Reported by At-Tabarani and Ad-Delmeyy.)

Abu Sa*id Al-Khudryy reports that Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, said, "Whoever hates “Umar, hates me and whoever loves ‘Umar, loves me. Allah boasts of people on the eve of “Arafaat in general and of “Umar in particular, Whenever Allah sent a prophet to a nation, he had a spokesman among his people. If there is one among my nation, he is ~Umar." He was asked, "Who is a spokesman, Allah's Messenger? He answered, "He is the mouthpiece of the angels." (Reported by At-Tabarani.)

It is reported that the Prophet, peace and blessings the upon him, saw ~Umar dressed in a garment (in another narration, a white raiment), so he asked him, "/s your garment new or washed?" ~Umar answered, "It is washed." The Prophet returned, "Wear new clothes, live praised (by people) all your life, and die as a martyr." (Reported by Ahmad and * Abdur-Raziq.) -

Anas Ibn Malik narrates: "The Prophet ascended the mountain of Uhud accompanied by Abu Bakr, ~Umar and >Uthmaan. The mountain shook beneath them. The Prophet hit it with his foot and said, 'O Uhud ! Be firm, for on you there is none but a Prophet, a Siddeeq and a martyr (i.e. and two martyrs).' " (Reported by Al-Bukhari, Abu Dawood and AtTarmidhi.)

Abu Hurayrah recounts that the Prophet said, "While a

man was riding a cow, it turned and said to him, ‘J have not been created for this purpose {i.e. carrying loads), I have been created for ploughing.’ " The Prophet added, "I, Abu Bakr and “Umar believe the story." The Prophet went on, "A wolf caught a sheep, and when the shepherd chased it, the wolf said, 'Who will be its guard on the day of wild beasts, when there will be no shepherd for it except me?' " Having narrated the story, the Prophet said, "I, Abu Bakr and ~Umar too believe it." Abu Salama (a sub-narrator) said, "Abu Bakr and “Umar were not present then." ( Reported by Ahmad, Al-Bukhari, Moslem and An-Nasaa’i.)

Ibn “Umar, may Allah be pleased with him and his father, reports that the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said: "The most merciful of my nation to my nation is Abu Bakr andthe firmest in Abiding by Allah's commands is “Umar...” (— Reported by Ahmad, At-Tarmidhi and AnNasaa’i.)

“Umar was second to none but Abu Bakr in closeness to and approval from the Prophet. He, peace and blessings be upon him, said: "J have two inhabitants from the inhabitants of heaven and two ministers from the inhabitants of earth. The former are Jibreel (Gabriel) and Mika'eel, and the latter are Abu Bakr and ~Umar." It is related that the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said: "Those two are like my ears and eyes;" he meant Abu Bakr and *Umar. (Reported by AtTarmidhi and Al-Hakim.)

The Prophet also said, "J do not know how much longer I shall live. So follow those who will outlive me: Abu Bakr and “Umar.” (Reported by At-Tarmidhi.)

The Prophet's Companions and Early Muslims Speak of Umar:

Abu Bakr As-Sideeq, may Allah be pleased with him, 155

said, "No man is dearer to me than * Umar." (Reported by Ibn ~ Aasaakir.)

Having fallen ill, Abu Bakr was asked, "What would you say to Allah, having appointed * Umar as your successor?" He answered, "I would say, | appointed the very best of the people as their ruler." (Reported by Ibn Sa*d.)

“Ali, may Allah be pleased with him, said, "If we are to mention the righteous, ‘Umar is surely first on the list. There was never any doubt that he was the voice of wisdom." (Reported by At-Tabarani.)

Ibn *~Umar, may Allah be pleased with him, said, "Ever since the demise of Allah's Messenger, I have not seen anyone firmer in faith or more generous than Umar.” (Reported by Ibn Sa*d.)

Ibn Mas‘ ood, may Allah be pleased with him, said, "If *Umar's knowledge was placed in one of the scales of the balance and the knowledge of all the people in the other, his would outweigh theirs. He was believed to possess nine tenths of knowledge." (Reported by At-Tabarani and Al-Hakim.)

Hudhayfah mentions, "Never have I known aman to fear no one in Abiding by Allah's laws except ~ Umar."

Mu‘awiyah, may Allah be pleased with him, states, "Life offered Abu Bakr no temptations and he never sought worldly pleasures either, whereas life did offer *Umar temptations, yet he never yielded to them. We, on the other hand, have been soaked in worldly temptations." (Reported by Az-Zubayr Ibn Bakkaar.)

Ibn Mas*ood, may Allah be pleased with him, said, " “Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab outdid people in four things; when he

thought the captives of the Badr battle should be killed, then Allah revealed His words:

CVA SWI ge a gs Gus YI"

"Had it not been for a previous ordainment from Allah" (AlAnfaal: 68) Concerning the veil, he ordered the Prophet's wives to stay in their houses. So Zaynab told him, 'O Ibn AlKhattaab, would you force us to obey your orders when Allah's revelation comes down in our homes?’ Then Allah revealed the following verse:

(OF rote My “Ea Sb di 11"

"And when ye ask (his ladies) for anything ye want," (AlAhzab: 53)

Thirdly, he did when the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, prayed, "O Allah, strengthen Islam with “Umar.” Finally, “Umar outdid people on account of his opinion of Abu Bakr, for he was the first to pledge allegiance to him." (Reported by Ahamd, Al-Bazzaar and At-Tabarani.)

Mujahid reports: "We were told that the devils were shackled during ~Umar's caliphate. They were not released until he was stabbed."

Instances When the Divine Revelation Concorded with ~Umar's View:

Those instances are generally believed to amount to twenty. Mujahid states: "“Umar's views were confirmed by the Quran." Ibn ~“Umar is reported to have said, "Whenever

people held a certain view about something and * Umar held another, the Quran was revealed to confirm his view."

“Umar is narrated to have said: "My Lord agreed with me in three things: Firstly, I said, 'O Allah's Messenger, I wish we took the Station of Abraham as our praying place (for some of our prayers).' So came the Divine Inspiration:

(NYO 8 Athy iad peal digi op isc *

"And take ye the Station of Abraham as a place of prayer." (Al-Bagarah: 125)

Secondly, as regards the verse of the veiling of women, I said, 'O Allah's Messenger! I wish you ordered your wives to cover themselves from men because good and bad people talk to them.’ So the verse of the veiling of women was revealed. Thirdly, once the Prophet's wives united against him, I told them:

"It may be, if he divorced you (all), that Allah will give him in exchange Consorts better than you," So this verse (the same as I had said) was revealed." (At-Tahreem: 5). (Reported by Al Bukhari and Moslem in their two authentic books.)

Similarly, Ibn ‘Umar, may Allah be pleased with him and his father, reports that ‘Umar said: "My Lord concorded with (my judgments) on three occasions. In the case of the Station of Abraham, in the case of the observance of the veil and in the case of the captives of Badr." (Reported by Moslem.) This Hadith therefore reveals a fourth instance.

According to AnNawawi's At-Tahdheeb, the Quran confirmed ~Umar's opinion concerning the captives of Badr, the veil, Abraham's Station, and the prohibition of wine. We hence learn of a fifth incident. ~ Umar is reported to have said, "O Allah, show us an indisputable evidence concerning wine." Allah thereupon prohibited it.

As for the sixth incident, Anas reports: “ ~ Umar said, ‘Allah's revelation concorded with my view as regards four things: when the following verse was revealed:

(VY Os jlly “ob oo DO oy Oy UE Ua"

"Man We did create from a quintessence (of clay)" (AlMu'minun: 12) I said, 'So blessed be Allah, the Best to create! So Allah revealed:

"So blessed be Allah, the Best to create!" (Al-Mu'minun: 14) (Reported by Ibn Abu Hatim.)

Seventhly, ~Umar stated: "When * Abdullaah Ibn 'Ubayy died, the Prophet was called to lead the prayer at his funeral. The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, got up. I stood up right in front of him to stop him, saying, 'O Allah's Messenger, will you pray at the funeral of Allah’s enemy, Ibn ‘Ubayy, who once said so and so?' I swear that it was only shortly afterwards that Allah sent down His words:

"Nor do thou ever pray for any of them that dies." (AtTawbah: 84)" ~Umar's Miracles:

Ibn “Umar, may Allah be pleased with him and his father, is reported to have said: "* Umar sent out an army, at the helm of which was a man called Saariyah. While * Umar was delivering a sermon, he called out three times, 'O Saariyah, seek the protection of the mount!’ When the messenger of the army arrived, ‘Umar asked him about the battle. The messenger answered: 'O Commander of the faithful, we were about to be defeated. At that point, we heard a voice call out three times, 'O Saariyah, seek the protection of the mount!’ So we assumed our positions in front of the mountain and Allah granted us victory. “Umar was told, "You really did cry so, though the mountain Saariyah was stationed by was at Nahawand, in the land of non-Muslims.'" (Reported by Al-Baihaqi and Abu Na*eem.)
content_id:
At-Tarmidhi juga meriwayatkan bahwa 'Uqbah Ibn 'Amir berkata, "Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda, 'Seandainya ada seorang Nabi setelahku, pastilah itu adalah 'Umar Ibn Al-Khattab.' "

Ibn 'Umar meriwayatkan, "Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda, ''Umar akan menjadi "bulan purnama" di antara para penghuni Surga.' " (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bazzar.)

Anas Ibn Malik dan 'Ali Ibn Abu Thalib, radhiyallahu 'anhuma, meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda kepada Abu Bakr dan 'Umar: "Keduanya akan menjadi pemimpin orang-orang tua di Surga, baik dari zaman dahulu maupun sekarang, kecuali para rasul dan para nabi... Jangan beritahukan hal itu kepada mereka, wahai 'Ali." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tarmidhi.)

'Aa'ishah, radhiyallahu 'anha, dikabarkan pernah berkata, "Setan merasa takut kepada 'Umar." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn 'Asakir.)

Ibn 'Abbas, radhiyallahu 'anhuma, meriwayatkan, "Jibril datang kepada Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, dan berkata, 'Sampaikan salam Allah kepada 'Umar dan beritahukan kepadanya bahwa kemarahannya (ketika Allah dilanggar) hanya memperkuat Islam, dan kepuasannya (terhadap sesuatu yang diucapkan atau dilakukan) menegaskan bahwa hal itu benar.' " (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Thabrani.)

Ibn 'Abbas, radhiyallahu 'anhuma,

lebih lanjut meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda, "Setiap malaikat di langit menghormati 'Umar, dan setiap setan di bumi takut kepadanya." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn 'Asakir.)

Al-Fadhl Ibn 'Abbas meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda, "Setelah wafatku, kebenaran akan selalu bersama 'Umar di mana pun ia berada." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Thabrani dan Ad-Deilami.)

Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda, "Barangsiapa membenci 'Umar, maka ia membenciku, dan barangsiapa mencintai 'Umar, maka ia mencintaiku. Allah membanggakan para hamba-Nya di malam 'Arafah secara umum dan 'Umar secara khusus. Setiap kali Allah mengutus seorang nabi kepada suatu umat, selalu ada seorang juru bicara di antara kaumnya. Jika ada satu orang di antara umatku, maka itu adalah 'Umar." Ditanyakan kepada beliau, "Siapakah juru bicara itu, ya Rasulullah?" Beliau menjawab, "Ia adalah penyambung lidah para malaikat." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Thabrani.)

Diriwayatkan bahwa Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, melihat 'Umar mengenakan sebuah pakaian (dalam riwayat lain, sehelai kain putih), maka beliau bertanya kepadanya, "Apakah pakaianmu itu baru atau sudah dicuci?" 'Umar menjawab, "Sudah dicuci." Nabi pun berkata, "Pakailah pakaian baru, jalani hidup dengan pujian (dari manusia) sepanjang hidupmu, dan matilah sebagai seorang syahid." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad dan 'Abdur-Raziq.)

Anas Ibn Malik meriwayatkan: "Nabi mendaki gunung Uhud bersama Abu Bakr, 'Umar, dan 'Utsman. Gunung itu berguncang di bawah mereka. Nabi menghentakkan kakinya ke gunung itu dan bersabda, 'Wahai Uhud! Tenanglah, karena di atasmu tidak ada kecuali seorang Nabi, seorang Siddiq, dan seorang syahid (yakni dua orang syahid).' " (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari, Abu Dawud, dan At-Tarmidhi.)

Abu Hurayrah menceritakan bahwa Nabi bersabda, "Ketika

seorang pria sedang menunggangi seekor sapi, sapi itu menoleh dan berkata kepadanya, 'Aku tidak diciptakan untuk tujuan ini (yaitu membawa beban), aku diciptakan untuk membajak.' " Nabi kemudian menambahkan, "Aku, Abu Bakr, dan 'Umar mempercayai kisah itu." Nabi melanjutkan, "Seekor serigala menangkap seekor domba, dan ketika si gembala mengejarnya, serigala itu berkata, 'Siapa yang akan menjaganya pada hari para binatang buas, ketika tidak ada gembala untuknya selain aku?' " Setelah menceritakan kisah itu, Nabi bersabda, "Aku, Abu Bakr, dan 'Umar pun mempercayainya." Abu Salamah (seorang sub-perawi) berkata, "Abu Bakr dan 'Umar tidak hadir saat itu." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad, Al-Bukhari, Muslim, dan An-Nasa'i.)

Ibn 'Umar, radhiyallahu 'anhu wa 'an abihi, meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda: "Yang paling penyayang terhadap umatku dari kalangan umatku adalah Abu Bakr, dan yang paling teguh dalam menjalankan perintah Allah adalah 'Umar..." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad, At-Tarmidhi, dan An-Nasa'i.)

'Umar tidak ada yang menyamainya kecuali Abu Bakr dalam hal kedekatan dan persetujuan dari Nabi. Beliau, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda: "Aku memiliki dua penghuni dari penghuni langit dan dua menteri dari penghuni bumi. Yang pertama adalah Jibril (Malaikat Jibril) dan Mikail, dan yang terakhir adalah Abu Bakr dan 'Umar." Diriwayatkan pula bahwa Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda: "Keduanya bagaikan telinga dan mataku;" yang beliau maksud adalah Abu Bakr dan 'Umar. (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tarmidhi dan Al-Hakim.)

Nabi juga bersabda, "Aku tidak tahu berapa lama lagi aku akan hidup. Maka ikutilah orang-orang yang akan bertahan setelahku: Abu Bakr dan 'Umar." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tarmidhi.)

Para Sahabat Nabi dan Kaum Muslimin Awal Berbicara tentang Umar:

Abu Bakr As-Siddiq, radhiyallahu 'anhu, 155

berkata, "Tidak ada orang yang lebih kucintai selain 'Umar." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn 'Asakir.)

Ketika Abu Bakr jatuh sakit, ia ditanya, "Apa yang akan kamu katakan kepada Allah, karena telah mengangkat 'Umar sebagai penggantimu?" Ia menjawab, "Aku akan berkata, aku telah mengangkat orang terbaik di antara mereka sebagai pemimpin mereka." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Sa'd.)

'Ali, radhiyallahu 'anhu, berkata, "Jika kita hendak menyebut orang-orang yang saleh, 'Umar pasti berada di urutan pertama dalam daftar itu. Tidak pernah ada keraguan bahwa dialah suara kebijaksanaan." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Thabrani.)

Ibn 'Umar, radhiyallahu 'anhu, berkata, "Sejak wafatnya Rasulullah, aku tidak pernah melihat seseorang yang lebih teguh imannya atau lebih dermawan daripada 'Umar." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Sa'd.)

Ibn Mas'ud, radhiyallahu 'anhu, berkata, "Jika ilmu 'Umar diletakkan di salah satu sisi timbangan dan ilmu seluruh manusia di sisi yang lain, ilmunya pasti akan lebih berat daripada ilmu mereka. Ia diyakini memiliki sembilan persepuluh dari seluruh ilmu." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Thabrani dan Al-Hakim.)

Hudhayfah menyebutkan, "Belum pernah aku mengetahui seorang pun yang tidak takut kepada siapa pun dalam menjalankan hukum-hukum Allah kecuali 'Umar."

Mu'awiyah, radhiyallahu 'anhu, menyatakan, "Kehidupan dunia tidak pernah menawarkan godaan kepada Abu Bakr dan ia pun tidak pernah mencari kesenangan duniawi, sementara kehidupan dunia memang menawarkan godaan kepada 'Umar, namun ia tidak pernah menyerah kepadanya. Sedangkan kita, telah terbenam dalam godaan duniawi." (Diriwayatkan oleh Az-Zubayr Ibn Bakkar.)

Ibn Mas'ud, radhiyallahu 'anhu, berkata, "'Umar Ibn Al-Khattab mengungguli manusia dalam empat hal; ketika ia

berpendapat bahwa para tawanan perang Badr seharusnya dibunuh, maka Allah menurunkan firman-Nya:

CVA SWI ge a gs Gus YI"

"Seandainya tidak ada ketetapan yang telah terdahulu dari Allah" (Al-Anfal: 68). Mengenai hijab, ia memerintahkan para istri Nabi untuk tinggal di rumah mereka. Maka Zaynab berkata kepadanya, 'Wahai Ibn Al-Khattab, apakah kamu akan memaksakan perintahmu kepada kami sementara wahyu Allah turun di rumah kami?' Lalu Allah menurunkan ayat berikut:

(OF rote My "Ea Sb di 11"

"Dan apabila kamu meminta sesuatu (keperluan) kepada mereka (istri-istri Nabi)," (Al-Ahzab: 53)

Ketiga, hal itu terjadi ketika Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, berdoa, "Ya Allah, kuatkanlah Islam dengan 'Umar." Terakhir, 'Umar mengungguli manusia berdasarkan pendapatnya tentang Abu Bakr, karena ia adalah orang pertama yang membaiatnya." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad, Al-Bazzar, dan At-Thabrani.)

Mujahid meriwayatkan: "Kami diberitahu bahwa para setan dibelenggu selama masa khilafah 'Umar. Mereka tidak dilepaskan hingga ia ditikam."

Contoh-contoh Ketika Wahyu Ilahi Sesuai dengan Pendapat 'Umar:

Contoh-contoh tersebut secara umum diyakini berjumlah dua puluh kali. Mujahid menyatakan: "Pendapat-pendapat 'Umar dikonfirmasi oleh Al-Quran." Ibn 'Umar dilaporkan pernah berkata, "Setiap kali

orang-orang memiliki pandangan tertentu tentang sesuatu dan 'Umar memiliki pandangan yang berbeda, maka Al-Quran diturunkan untuk mengonfirmasi pandangannya."

'Umar diriwayatkan pernah berkata: "Rabb-ku menyetujuiku dalam tiga hal: Pertama, aku berkata, 'Ya Rasulullah, alangkah baiknya jika kita menjadikan Maqam Ibrahim sebagai tempat shalat (untuk beberapa shalat kita).' Maka turunlah Wahyu Ilahi:

(NYO 8 Athy iad peal digi op isc *

"Dan jadikanlah sebagian Maqam Ibrahim sebagai tempat shalat." (Al-Baqarah: 125)

Kedua, mengenai ayat tentang hijab bagi wanita, aku berkata, 'Ya Rasulullah! Alangkah baiknya jika engkau memerintahkan para istrimu untuk menutup diri dari kaum pria, karena orang baik maupun orang jahat berbicara kepada mereka.' Maka turunlah ayat tentang hijab bagi wanita. Ketiga, suatu ketika para istri Nabi bersatu menentang beliau, aku berkata kepada mereka:

"Jika ia menceraikan kamu sekalian, mudah-mudahan Rabb-nya akan memberinya ganti istri-istri yang lebih baik dari kamu," Maka ayat ini (sama seperti yang telah aku katakan) pun diturunkan." (At-Tahrim: 5). (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dan Muslim dalam dua kitab hadith shahih mereka.)

Demikian pula, Ibn 'Umar, radhiyallahu 'anhu wa 'an abihi, meriwayatkan bahwa 'Umar berkata: "Rabb-ku menyetujui (hukum-hukumku) dalam tiga kejadian. Dalam kasus Maqam Ibrahim, dalam kasus pelaksanaan hijab, dan dalam kasus para tawanan perang Badr." (Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim.) Hadith ini dengan demikian mengungkapkan kejadian keempat.

Menurut At-Tahdheeb karya An-Nawawi, Al-Quran membenarkan pendapat 'Umar mengenai para tawanan Badr, hijab, Maqam Ibrahim, dan pengharaman khamr. Maka kita mengetahui insiden kelima. Diriwayatkan bahwa 'Umar berkata, "Ya Allah, tunjukkanlah kepada kami bukti yang tak terbantahkan mengenai khamr." Allah pun kemudian mengharamkannya.

Adapun insiden keenam, Anas meriwayatkan: "'Umar berkata, 'Wahyu Allah bersesuaian dengan pandanganku dalam empat hal: ketika ayat berikut diturunkan:

(VY Os jlly "ob oo DO oy Oy UE Ua"

"Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah menciptakan manusia dari suatu saripati (berasal) dari tanah" (Al-Mu'minun: 12) Aku berkata, 'Maka Mahasuci Allah, Pencipta yang paling baik!' Maka Allah menurunkan:

"Maka Mahasuci Allah, Pencipta yang paling baik!" (Al-Mu'minun: 14) (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Abu Hatim.)

Ketujuh, 'Umar menyatakan: "Ketika 'Abdullah bin Ubay meninggal, Nabi dipanggil untuk memimpin shalat jenazahnya. Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam pun berdiri. Aku berdiri tepat di hadapannya untuk mencegahnya, seraya berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah, apakah engkau akan menshalati musuh Allah, Ibnu Ubay, yang pernah berkata demikian dan demikian?' Aku bersumpah bahwa tidak lama setelah itu Allah menurunkan firman-Nya:

"Dan janganlah engkau sekali-kali menyalatkan (jenazah) seorang pun di antara mereka yang mati." (At-Taubah: 84)" Karamah-Karamah 'Umar:

Diriwayatkan bahwa Ibnu 'Umar radhiyallahu 'anhuma berkata: "'Umar pernah mengirim pasukan, yang dipimpin oleh seorang lelaki bernama Saariyah. Ketika 'Umar sedang menyampaikan khutbah, ia berseru tiga kali, 'Wahai Saariyah, berlindunglah ke gunung!' Ketika utusan pasukan itu tiba, 'Umar bertanya kepadanya tentang pertempuran tersebut. Utusan itu menjawab: 'Wahai Amirul Mukminin, kami hampir saja dikalahkan. Pada saat itu, kami mendengar sebuah suara berseru tiga kali, 'Wahai Saariyah, berlindunglah ke gunung!' Maka kami pun mengambil posisi di depan gunung dan Allah memberikan kami kemenangan.' 'Umar pun diberitahu, 'Engkau memang benar-benar berseru demikian, padahal gunung tempat Saariyah bermarkas berada di Nahawand, di negeri kaum non-Muslim.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Baihaqi dan Abu Na'im.)
content_ar: At-Tarmidhi أيضاً reports ~Uqbah Ibn ~Amir to have said, "الله's Messenger, peace and البركات be upon him, said, ‘إذا there be a النبي بعد me, that would be “عمر Ibn Al-Khattaab.' " Ibn “عمر narrates, "النبي ﷺ, peace and البركات be upon him, said, ' ‘عمر will be the "bright moon" among the residents of الجنة.’ “ (Reported by Al-Bazzaar.) أنس Ibn مالك and ~ علي Ibn Abu Taalib, may الله be pleased with them both, narrate that الله's Messenger, peace and البركات be upon him, said to أبو بكر and * عمر: "Those two will be the chiefs of the old men in الجنة, both from old and modern times, excluding الرسل and الأنبياء... Do not tell them of that, “علي.” (Reported by AtTarmidhi.) *Aa’ishah, may الله be pleased with her, is reported to have said, “ الشيطان fears ~ عمر." (Reported by Ibn ~ Aasaakir.) Ibn ~Abbaas, may الله be pleased with them both, narrates, "جبريل ﷺ came to النبي ﷺ, peace and البركات be upon him, and said, ‘Give “عمر الله's greetings and tell him that his rage (عندما الله is disobeyed) only boosts الإسلام, and his satisfaction (with something said or done) asserts that it is correct.’ " (Reported by At-Tabarani.) Ibn ~Abbaas, may الله be pleased with them both, further reports that النبي ﷺ, peace and البركات be upon him, said, "Every angel in heaven reveres “عمر, and every الشيطان on earth fears him." (Reported by Ibn ~ Aasaakir.) Al-Fadl, Ibn ~ Abbaas reports that النبي ﷺ, peace and البركات be upon him, said, "بعد my decease, the الحق shall be with “عمر wherever he may be." (Reported by At-Tabarani and Ad-Delmeyy.) Abu Sa*id Al-Khudryy reports that الله's Messenger, peace and البركات be upon him, said, "Whoever hates “عمر, hates me and whoever loves ‘عمر, loves me. الله boasts of people on the eve of “Arafaat in general and of “عمر وبالأخص, كلما الله sent a النبي to a nation, he had a spokesman among his people. إذا there is one among my nation, he is ~عمر." He was asked, "Who is a spokesman, الله's Messenger? رد, "He is the mouthpiece of the الملائكة." (Reported by At-Tabarani.) وردت الرواية أنه النبي ﷺ, peace and البركات the upon him, saw ~عمر dressed in a garment (in another narration, a white raiment), لذا he asked him, "/s your garment new or washed?" ~عمر answered, "It is washed." النبي ﷺ returned, "Wear new clothes, live praised (by people) all your الحياة, and die as a martyr." (Reported by أحمد and * Abdur-Raziq.) - أنس Ibn مالك narrates: "النبي ﷺ ascended the mountain of Uhud accompanied by أبو بكر, ~عمر and >Uthmaan. The mountain shook beneath them. النبي ﷺ hit it with his foot and said, 'O Uhud ! Be firm, for on you there is none but a النبي, a Siddeeq and a martyr (i.e. and two martyrs).' " (Reported by البخاري, أبو داود and AtTarmidhi.) أبو هريرة recounts that النبي ﷺ said, "بينما a man was riding a cow, it t
